chunk_summary: Visit one in obesity management involves diagnostic confirmation, severity classification, etiologic triage, comorbidity identification, and initial treatment planning, using structured anthropometric and clinical guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the structured approach to diagnostic confirmation and severity classification in pediatric obesity.
Learn to perform etiologic triage and identify cases requiring specialized referral.
Recognize red-flag comorbidities that necessitate urgent intervention.
Develop an initial treatment plan with actionable goals and follow-up cadence.
key_facts: Use WHO weight-for-length/height charts for children under 5 years and IAP 2015 BMI charts for ages 5–18 years.
Overweight is defined as an adult-equivalent BMI of 23, and obesity as an adult-equivalent BMI of 27.
Class 2 and class 3 obesity are determined using the IAP percent-of-cutoff approach.
Red-flag comorbidities include stage 2 hypertension, random glucose ≥200 mg/dL, SCFE, severe OSA, and suicidality.
Avoid broad endocrine testing in typical obesity to prevent false thyroid diagnoses and care delays.
reference_quote: Avoid ordering broad endocrine tests in typical obesity; this commonly creates false thyroid labels and delays care. (AAP Clinical Practice Guideline, 2023)
